commit_message: good progress on front end, organized the js files a bit, password and memory reset implemented

user_prompt: during password reset through the web interface, I get `N.Pbkdf2HmacSha256 is not a function` as the error, something is wrong with the webcrypt shim?
agent_reply: Yep, that was the shim. Its PBKDF2 path tries to call `asmCrypto.Pbkdf2HmacSha256`, but the bundled shim does not actually provide that function, so password reset dies before it can POST the new key.

I fixed [sec_helpers.js](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/sec_helpers.js:33) so `derive_pbkdf2_bytes()` still tries WebCrypto first, but falls back to a local JS PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 implementation if the shim/native path fails. I also rebuilt the packaged web asset in [web_assets.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h:3).
Verified the fallback against Node’s `crypto.pbkdf2Sync` for known vectors, then built:
Both passed. Sec0 had one transient stale `.pio` object failure on the first try, then passed cleanly on retry.

commit_message: fixed encrypted recording being too short
